name: Ambiguous Multi-Type Input — Lab Equipment Booking System
input: I need a diagram for our lab equipment booking system. There are several states an equipment reservation can be in: Available, Reserved, In Use, Under Maintenance, Calibration Required, Decommissioned. Transitions: Available -> Reserved (researcher books). Reserved -> In Use (researcher checks in). In Use -> Available (researcher checks out, equipment passes QC). In Use -> Calibration Required (usage hours exceed threshold). Calibration Required -> Under Maintenance (technician starts calibration). Under Maintenance -> Available (calibration complete, passes QC). Under Maintenance -> Decommissioned (failed calibration, beyond repair). Reserved -> Available (reservation cancelled or expired). Any state except Decommissioned can go to Under Maintenance for emergency repairs. Decommissioned is a terminal state. Use stateDiagram-v2.
qualityCriteria: All 6 states present (Available, Reserved, In Use, Under Maintenance, Calibration Required, Decommissioned)
Reserved to In Use transition present
In Use to Available (checkout) transition present
Calibration Required path from In Use
Under Maintenance to Available (calibration complete) path
Decommissioned as terminal state (no outgoing transitions except possibly [*])
Emergency maintenance transitions (multiple states to Under Maintenance)
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: State diagram with 6 states, emergency maintenance from multiple states, decommissioned terminal, calibration cycle.
